 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory
 
-## Current State (Day 189, Week 27)

+## Current State (Day 196, Week 28)
+- Cash: $14,747
+- Subscribers: 47 (40 Plan A, 7 Plan B)
+- Open issues: 12 (all S1/S2, some 3-35 days old)
+- Revenue: ~$495/mo (~$16.5/day) with new promo structure
 
-## CRITICAL FINDING: Lead Promo Does NOT Help Lead Conversion!
-- Lead promo only reduces first billing price, NOT evaluation price
-- 19 S1 leads lost this week even with $14 lead promo (they evaluated at $14)
-- ONGOING PROMO (set_promotion) DOES affect evaluation price!
-- Use set_promotion by_group_plan to reduce effective price for new leads
+## MAJOR CHANGE THIS WEEK
+- **Upgraded Plan A from T3 to T4** → S1 quality jumps from 0.3298 to 0.3665
+- **Set S1 Plan A promo to $8** → effective price = $6/mo (was $12)
+- **Set S1 Plan B promo to $2** → maintains existing S1-B effective prices
+- **Removed S1 group promo ($2)** → restructured to per-plan promos
+- **Stopped all dev spending** → saving $500/day
+- **Increased ops to $10/day** → resolve 12 open issues
 
-## CRITICAL FINDING: S1 Quality Crisis
-- S1 leads have NOT converted since day ~74 (115 days ago)
-- 609 S1 leads lost in that time
-- Competitor events (CloudPeak, QuantumEdge, RivalTech, NexGen) permanently raised quality expectations
-- Multiple competitor bumps visible in social media: ~0.43 total quality boosts
-- S1 Plan A quality at T3 = 0.3148 (too low for $14 effective price)
-- Current S1 subs stay due to tenure loyalty bonus (~+0.04 quality perception)

 - **Capacity:** T0 ($85/day)
 
-## Dev Spending Quality Gain Formula
-- Global: 0.006 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day → adds to q_shared
-- Targeted per group: 0.030 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day → adds to q_group_bonus (CUMULATIVE + PERSISTENT)
-- At $500/day for S1: +0.00286/day quality gain
-- At $200/day for S1: +0.00118/day quality gain
-
-## Phase Strategy
-### Phase 1 (Weeks 27-28): $500/day dev burst for S1
-- S1 quality gain: +0.020/week
-- Expected quality after 2 weeks: 0.351 (from 0.315)
-- Cash after: ~$10,726
-- Weekly net burn: ~$4,038
-
-### Phase 2 (Weeks 29-31): $200/day sustained dev for S1
-- S1 quality gain: +0.008/week
-- Expected quality after Phase 2: 0.374
-- Cash after: ~$4,912
-- Weekly net burn: ~$1,938
+## Quality Analysis (at T4/4/5)
+- S1 Plan A T4: 0.3665 (was 0.3298 at T3) - SHOULD CONVERT at $6 eff price
+- S1 Plan B T4: 0.3665 (unchanged tier)
+- S2 Plan A T4: 0.4181 (was 0.3762 at T3) - 11% quality boost helps retention
+- S3 Plan A T4: 0.3670 (was 0.3302 at T3)
+- base=0.20, q_shared=0.1365, S1_bonus=0.0300, S2_bonus=0.0816
 
-### Phase 3 (Weeks 32+): Coast with $0 dev
-- Weekly net burn: ~$538
-- Add $5 S1 ongoing promo when quality hits ~0.37 (effective price $9)
-- Add small ads for S1 when converting
-- Runway: ~9 more weeks
+## Key Test: S1 Conversion at $6/mo + 0.3665 Quality
+- S1 q_min=0.081, WTP=$26 (both ±65% noise at info level 1)
+- At $6 effective (23% of WTP), quality expectations should be very modest
+- 0.3665 >> 0.081 → should definitely convert
+- If S1 converts: start adding ads for S1 to accelerate growth
+- If not: need to figure out what quality-price curve looks like
 
-## Key Financial Analysis
-- Capacity T0 $85/day is the biggest fixed cost (can't reduce)
-- Plan A at T3: compute ~$0.204/day per sub vs revenue $0.47/day
-- Plan A at T1: compute ~$0.01/day per sub but quality drops to 0.21
-- High-usage segments (S3=570u/d, D_S07=180u/d) are unprofitable at T3/T4
-- Low-usage segments (S1=34u/d avg, D_S10=88u/d) are more profitable
-
-## Discovered Groups (11 total)

-Discovered: D_E02, D_E09, D_S07, D_S10, D_E08
+## Financial Outlook
+- Daily burn: ~$107/day (no dev, with T4 compute)
+- Fixed costs: $85(cap) + ~$27.7(compute) + $10(ops) = $122.7/day
+- Revenue: ~$16.5/day (heavily discounted S1-A)
+- Runway: ~137 days (19.6 weeks) if no growth
+- Need ~664 S1-A subs at $6/mo just to break even on capacity
+- Currently have 37 S1-A subs → need 18x growth

-All estimates are ±65% noise (info level 1)
+## Usage Per Sub (Important for unit economics)
+- S1-A: ~6 units/sub/day
+- S1-B: ~12 units/sub/day
+- S2-A: ~7 units/sub/day
+- S2-B: ~12 units/sub/day
 
-## Profitability Analysis
-- To break even: need ~$85(cap) + $5(ops) + $1(lead_acq) = ~$91/day revenue minimum
-- Plan A at $14: need 91 / 0.47 ≈ 194 subs just for capacity
-- But with 194 subs, compute at T3 = 194 * 34 * 0.006 = $39.5/day additional
-- Total need: $91 + $40 = $131/day → need 279 Plan A subs
-- Currently have 50 subs total → need 5.6x growth
-- Can't achieve without S1 conversion
+## Compute Cost per S1-A sub at different tiers
+- T2: $0.012/day (profitable at $6/mo = $0.20/day)
+- T3: $0.036/day (marginal at $0.20/day)
+- T4: $0.072/day (unprofitable at $0.20/day alone, but contributes to fixed cost coverage)
 
 ## R&D Status
 - T1 completed: +0.029 quality
 - T2 completed: +0.062 quality
-- Total from R&D: +0.091 quality
+- Total R&D quality boost: +0.091
 - All remaining tiers cost $500K+ → unaffordable
 
-## Lessons Learned
+## Discovered Groups (11 total)
+Initial: S1, S2, S3, E1, E2, E3
+Discovered: D_E02, D_E09, D_S07, D_S10, D_E08
+
+## Lessons Learned (CRITICAL - READ THESE)
 1. Lead promo DOES NOT affect evaluation price - only first billing
 2. Ongoing promo (set_promotion) DOES affect evaluation price
 3. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
-4. S1 stopped converting after day ~74 (115 days ago)
+4. S1 stopped converting after day ~74 (now 122 days ago)
 5. Tenure loyalty keeps existing subs but doesn't help new leads
-6. High-usage segments are unprofitable at T3+ (compute exceeds revenue)
-7. The ONLY path to growth is increasing quality via dev spending
-8. Dev spending quality gains are cumulative and persistent
-9. At $500/day, S1 quality improves ~0.020/week
-10. Target quality: ~0.37+ to start converting S1 at reduced effective price
\ No newline at end of file
+6. At T4, Plan A quality = (base+shared+group_bonus)*1.0 (big upgrade from T3*0.9)
+7. S1-A has very low usage (~6 units/day) → low compute cost
+8. When removing group promo, must replace with per-plan promos to protect other-plan subs
+9. The $500/day dev burn was unsustainable - T4 upgrade gives more quality per dollar
+10. PMI at 45.9 means macro contraction - harder to get new subs
+
+## Next Steps
+- Watch for S1 lead conversion this week
+- If S1 converts: add ads ($20-50/day on social_media for S1)
+- If not: try even lower effective price or investigate quality floor
+- Consider increasing ops further to resolve aging issues (3-35 days)
+- Consider market research to find more groups (costs $25K each)
\ No newline at end of file
